title: Add role-based rendering logic to /dashboard/internal

requirements.text: Update `frontend/src/pages/dashboard/internal.tsx` to implement role-based rendering: if the current role is 'Admin' or 'Superadmin' (isSuperAdmin), render dashboards for ALL roles in a toggleable accordion (one accordion per role, toggled via `toggleRoleAccordion`); otherwise, only render the dashboard section for the currently logged-in role.

quotes:
const isSuperAdmin = role === "Admin" || role === "Superadmin";
  const rolesToRender = isSuperAdmin ? roles : [role];

acceptanceCriteria: When role is 'Admin' or 'Superadmin', all roles are rendered as separate accordion sections
Each accordion section header shows the role name (e.g., 'AdminUser Dashboard') and is clickable to toggle open/closed via toggleRoleAccordion
When role is neither 'Admin' nor 'Superadmin', only the section for that specific role is rendered (no accordion toggle header shown)
Role accordion state is managed with an openRoles map per role key

requirements.text: Within each role section in `frontend/src/pages/dashboard/internal.tsx`, render three sub-sections: (1) a grid of Summary Cards via `renderSummaryCards(r)`, (2) a Middle Section ('Details / List Section') toggled by clicking via `toggleSection(r, 'middle')` and conditionally rendering `renderMiddleSection(r)`, and (3) a Bottom Section ('History / Tickets Section') toggled by clicking via `toggleSection(r, 'bottom')` and conditionally rendering `renderBottomSection(r)`.
acceptanceCriteria: Summary Cards section renders a 4-column grid (md:grid-cols-4) for each role using renderSummaryCards(r)
Middle Section is visible and clickable; clicking toggles sections[r].middle; content renders only when sections[r].middle is true
Bottom Section is visible and clickable; clicking toggles sections[r].bottom; content renders only when sections[r].bottom is true
Both Middle and Bottom sections use 'bg-white shadow rounded p-4 cursor-pointer' styling
Section toggle state is managed per role in a sections map keyed by role name

requirements.text: Ensure the `frontend/src/App.tsx` route for `/dashboard/internal` passes a valid role prop (e.g., 'Admin' or 'Superadmin') to `InternalDashboard` so that the admin accordion view can be tested end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: The /dashboard/internal route renders InternalDashboard with a role that covers the admin/superadmin case for visual testing
Changing the role prop to a non-admin value (e.g., 'Concierge') should render only that role's dashboard section

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/hooks/ or frontend/src/components/ui/
Keep all dashboard logic within frontend/src/pages/dashboard/internal.tsx
Backend (backend/main.mo) should not be changed — this is a frontend-only update
nonGoals: Connecting the dashboard to live backend/canister data
Implementing real authentication or role resolution from Internet Identity
Adding new roles beyond the existing four (AdminUser, AdminFinance, Asistenmu, Concierge)
